title: Devices and methods for optimized spinal fixation
abstract: Devices and methods are provided for optimized spinal fixation using additive manufacturing techniques to create implants with optimized structure for various surgical approaches, anatomies, etc. One exemplary embodiment includes a cage having an X-shaped connection that can bear a load during cage impaction. The cage can be additively manufactured to incorporate features such as variable wall thickness or material density to adjust properties of the cage, including load bearing capability, flexibility, radiolucency, etc. The cage can further include one or more of the connectors disposed between upper and lower endplates. In some embodiments, the cage can include a feature for coupling an insertion device thereto for introducing the cage into the body of a patient. In some embodiments, a plate can be appended to or integrally formed with a proximal end of the cage to assist with securing the cage to vertebral bodies.
claims: Claims 1. A spinal implant, comprising: an upper plate; a lower plate; a tapered end extending between the upper plate and the lower plate that is configured for insertion into intervertebral space; a second end opposite the tapered end; and a plurality of struts extending laterally between the tapered end and the second end. 2. The implant of claim 1, further comprising a connector extending between the upper plate and the lower plate. 3. The implant of claim 2, wherein the connector is disposed within an interior volume defined by the implant. 4. The implant of any preceding claim, further comprising a connector extending between any of the upper plate and the lower plate and one or more of the plurality of struts. 5. The implant of claim 4, wherein the connector extending between any of the upper plate and the lower plate and one or more of the plurality of struts is formed along a perimeter wall of the implant. 6. The implant of claim 1, further comprising a connector configured to support loads applied to the upper end plate and the lower end plate. 7. The implant of any of claims 2 to 6, wherein the connector or connectors each define a hollow inner lumen. 8. The implant of any of claims 2 to 6, wherein the connector or connectors are each formed of a porous mesh. 9. The implant of any preceding claim, wherein at least a portion of the implant is formed of a porous mesh configured to promote osteointegration. 10. The implant of claim 9, wherein the portion of the implant is formed of any of titanium, polyetherketoneketone (PEKK), a ceramic, and a nickel titanium alloy. 1 1. The implant of any preceding claim, wherein any of the upper plate and the lower plate are curved such that a distance between the upper plate and the lower plate varies along the implant. 12. The implant of any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the upper plate and the lower plate include a roughened surface configured to promote bone fusion. 13. The implant of any preceding claim, wherein the implant is formed of a porous titanium mesh having reduced stiffness and increased radiolucency compared to solid titanium. 14. The implant of any preceding claim, wherein at least two of the plurality of struts cross to form an X-shaped connection extending along a sidewall of the implant between the upper plate, lower plate, tapered end, and second end. 15. The implant of any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the upper plate and lower plate includes one or more surface features configured to prevent migration of the implant. 16. The implant of any preceding claim, wherein an orifice is formed in the second end of the implant. 17. The implant of claim 16, further comprising a plate coupled to the second end of the implant, the plate including a plurality of through-holes. 18. The implant of claim 16, further comprising a plate integrally formed with the second end of the implant, the plate including a plurality of through-holes. 19. The implant of claim 17 or claim 18, wherein one of the plurality of through-holes in the plate aligns with the orifice formed in the second end of the implant. 20. The implant of any of claims 17 to 19, wherein one of the plurality of through-holes is disposed any of above the upper plate and below the lower plate. 21. The implant of any of claims 17 to 20, wherein the plate couples to the implant by interfacing with surface features formed on first and second sidewalls of the second end of the implant that are disposed on opposite sides of the orifice. 22. A method for spinal fixation, comprising: determining characteristics of an intervertebral space of a patient; additively manufacturing a spinal implant based on the determined characteristics of the intervertebral space, the spinal implant being defined by an upper plate, a lower plate, a tapered end extending between the upper plate and the lower plate, a second end opposite the tapered end, and a plurality of struts extending laterally between the tapered end and the second end; and inserting the spinal implant into the intervertebral space. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein characteristics of the intervertebral space include curvature of adjacent vertebrae and additively manufacturing the spinal implant includes forming at least one of the upper and lower plates with curvature that matches the curvature of the adjacent vertebrae. 24. The method of claim 22 or claim 23, wherein additively manufacturing a spinal implant based on the determined characteristics of the intervertebral space includes shaping one or more struts to form a wall of the spinal implant extending from the tapered end to the second end. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein a plurality of struts are shaped into an X-shaped pattern to form the wall of the spinal implant extending from the tapered end to the second end. 26. The method of any of claims 22 to 25, wherein determining the characteristics of the intervertebral space includes imaging the intervertebral space with any of MRI, CT, ultrasound, or optical imagers. 27. The method of any of claims 22 to 26, wherein additively manufacturing a spinal implant based on the determined characteristics of the intervertebral space includes reducing a density of material used to form the implant based on any of desired stiffness and strength. 28. The method of any of claims 22 to 27, wherein the material used to form the implant is any of titanium, polyetherketoneketone (PEKK), a ceramic, and a nickel titanium alloy. 29. The method of any of claims 22 to 28, further comprising coupling a plate member to the spinal implant to secure the spinal implant to at least one adjacent vertebra using one or more bone anchors. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the plate member is secured to both vertebrae adjacent the spinal implant. 31. The method of any of claims 22 to 30, further comprising securing the spinal implant to at least one adjacent vertebra using one or more bone anchors inserted through a plate member that is integrally formed with the spinal implant. 32. The method of any of claims 22 to 31, further comprising delivering bone growth- promoting materials into an interior of the spinal implant to enhance and expedite integration of the implant into a subjectâs body.
